key_insight: S-Adam stabilizes non-smooth deep learning optimization by detecting local geometric instability and dampening steps where gradient signals conflict.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical optimizer for quantization-aware training and noisy small-batch regimes where standard Adam can oscillate or converge poorly.
method: Estimate Local Geometric Instability (LGI) from variance in randomized directional derivatives.
Apply adaptive damping exp(-λρ) to shrink updates in high-instability regions.
Use a Clarke subdifferential-based convergence framework to justify stable non-smooth optimization.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports improved performance over AdamW and Prox-SGD, with up to 6% accuracy gain on CIFAR-100 and 3% on TinyImageNet while reducing gradient oscillations.
watch_item: The randomized probing and instability estimation add computation and hyperparameter sensitivity, so efficiency and tuning in large-scale training may be a concern.
paper_title: Singularity-aware Optimization via Randomized Geometric Probing: Towards Stable Non-smooth Optimization
